aboutHistoryText1: KSC Wiedikon was founded in 1982 in Zurich's Wiedikon district as a dedicated volleyball club. A group of enthusiastic athletes from the neighbourhood came together to pursue their shared passion. What began with a single men's team in the lowest league quickly grew into a staple of Zurich's recreational sports scene. In its early years, the club was able to steadily build new teams thanks to committed members and strong youth development.
aboutHistoryText2: In the early 1990s, KSC Wiedikon expanded its offering to include basketball — a step that would leave a lasting mark on the club. The combination of two indoor sports under one roof proved unique in Zurich and attracted a growing number of members over the years. Today, KSC Wiedikon counts over 300 active members in more than 20 teams, from regional leagues to recreational sport, making it one of the largest multi-sport clubs in Zurich's District 3. The close ties to the Wiedikon neighbourhood, the family-like atmosphere, and the passion for sport remain the foundation of the club.
aboutCommunityText: At KSC Wiedikon, togetherness is at the heart of everything we do. Whether on or off the court — we are one team. New members are warmly welcomed and quickly find their place in our diverse club family.
aboutFairplayText: Respect towards opponents, referees, and teammates is a given for us. We always compete fairly — in training, in matches, and in club life.
aboutDevelopmentText: From the first trial training to the regional league, we foster the athletic and personal development of our members. Our coaches rely on modern methods and individual support.
aboutHallUtogrundText: Our main hall for volleyball and basketball. Centrally located in Wiedikon with excellent public transport connections.
aboutHallLavaterText: Second training hall, primarily used for youth teams and additional training sessions.
aboutHallSihlfeldText: Third hall for basketball training and home games. Spacious court with spectator seating.

calendarDescription: In the KSCW calendar you'll find all games, training sessions, club events, and hall reservations. The full calendar with filters by team and sport is available on our members' platform.
calendarPlatformInfo: The full KSCW calendar is available on our members' platform. There you can filter by team, subscribe to individual events, and confirm your attendance.
calendarSubscribeDescription: Subscribe to the KSCW calendar directly in your calendar app (Google Calendar, Apple Calendar, Outlook). This way you'll always have all games and training sessions up to date on your phone.
calendarVolleyballDescription: All volleyball games and training sessions for the 2025/26 season.
calendarBasketballDescription: All basketball games and training sessions for the 2025/26 season.
footerDescription: Basketball and Volleyball since 1982. Sports club in Zurich-Wiedikon with over 30 teams across all age groups.
membershipTeamSpiritText: At KSCW, togetherness comes first. Whether beginner or experienced player — you'll find your team with us. Training, winning, and celebrating together: that's the KSCW spirit.
membershipTrainingText: Our experienced coaches offer structured training sessions for all skill levels. Multiple times per week in well-equipped halls in Zurich-Wiedikon.
membershipCommunityText: KSCW is more than a sports club. Club events, group outings, and an active community make us a family — on and off the court.
membershipCategoryActiveDesc: 18 years and older, playing rights included
membershipCategoryFamilyDesc: For families with multiple active members
membershipFeesNote: All fees are per season (September to June). The federation licence (Swiss Volley / Swiss Basketball) is not included and will be charged separately.
membershipRegistrationText: Becoming a KSCW member is simple. Just three easy steps:
membershipStep1Text: Come to a training session with no obligation and get to know the team. You can attend up to three trial sessions before deciding. Simply sign up via the contact form or email.
membershipStep2Text: After the trial training, you'll receive the registration form from the coach. Fill it in and hand it in directly or send it by email to the board.
membershipStep3Text: You'll receive a confirmation email with all further information — access to the members' platform, training times, and contact persons for your team.
schedulingIntroText1: KSC Wiedikon's game scheduling enables efficient coordination of home games. Our teams play their home games on designated game Saturdays, where multiple teams can play on the same day in different time slots. The scheduling automatically considers hall availability, referee assignments, and conflicts.
schedulingIntroText2: Opposing teams can book their preferred game dates directly through our online tool. Confirmation is sent automatically via email.
schedulingSaturdaysText: Most home games take place on game Saturdays. Three time slots are available per Saturday, spread across our halls. This allows multiple teams to play simultaneously or consecutively.
schedulingOpponentsText: As an opposing team, you can book your home game dates with us directly online. You'll receive a personalised link to the scheduling tool from your KSCW contact. There you can see all available dates and time slots and select your preferred date.
schedulingStep1: You receive a personal booking link via email
schedulingStep2: Select one of the available game Saturdays and a time slot
schedulingStep3: Enter your contact details and any preferences
schedulingStep4: You receive an automatic confirmation via email
schedulingStep5: In case of conflicts, our scheduler will contact you directly
schedulingHallsText: KSC Wiedikon uses three gyms in the Wiedikon neighbourhood for training and home games. All halls are easily accessible by public transport.
